title: poa pratensis subsp. pruinosa
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. pruinosa, also known as meadow grass, is a subspecies of the Kentucky bluegrass plant, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Poa pratensis subsp. pruinosa lie in its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in herbal medicine. Poa pratensis subsp. pruinosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in herbal supplements and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
